dimensions.note: Deliberately unchanged: clients stay ambient-only (new RDSClient({region, ...SHARED_RETRY}), no credentials argument, no profile support added — retry alignment only per the task scope). The new aws_credentials twin is consumed solely for SHARED_RETRY; its credentialModeField/selectCredentials/preflight helpers are present but unused (bundled dead weight accepted to keep the twin byte-identical with the canonical module). The known credentialModeField 'looks like a secret' WARN is the documented false positive.

Entry/progress/completion info logs unchanged. ACCEPTED TELEMETRY LOSS (grilled decision): the debug-level 'Throttled on {op}' retry log went away with retryDeps/withRetry — the SDK adaptive retry has no equivalent hook. No sensitive values logged.

Previously this package had ONLY the app-level withRetry (no SDK retry config); now the RDS client carries SHARED_RETRY (adaptive, maxAttempts 3) as the single retry mechanism. A throttle that survives the client's bounded retries propagates and fails the method (this model has no scan_error spec — the method-level failure is the designed surface, unchanged). Pinned by a new collectInstances test: a throttle propagates with exactly one send (no app-level re-issue).

The withRetry/isThrottlingError suites (and their smoke-test existence assertions) were deleted with the module. Surviving observable behavior keeps real teeth: collectInstances still paginates each batch to exhaustion by Marker, chunks at MAX_CLUSTER_IDS_PER_FILTER, skips id-less instances, issues zero calls for empty input, and (new) propagates a throttle without an app-level re-issue. collectInstances' unused logger parameter was dropped alongside; all call sites and tests updated.

list_clusters remains read-only and re-runnable with deterministic cluster/instance keys. Retry-semantics change (20 full-jitter app attempts -> SDK adaptive 3) is the accepted trade-off: a sustained throttle fails the run sooner instead of stalling up to 20x90s per page; adaptive client-side rate limiting backs off instead of hammering.

No AWS request/response mapping changed; DescribeDBClusters/DescribeDBInstances pagination and the db-cluster-id server-side filter unchanged; MaxRecords 100; SDK pins unchanged at 3.1073.0. Rate limiting now handled by the SDK adaptive limiter on the client. collectInstances' signature changed (logger parameter removed) — an intentional API reduction shipped with the version bump.
No new clients, handles, or files; client construction gains only the retry config spread. Cleanup behavior unchanged.

Manifest and model.version aligned at 2026.07.03.0. README Throttling section rewritten for the SDK adaptive retry as the single mechanism (the withRetry/full-jitter/20-attempts text is gone). Test fixtures keep placeholder identifiers (cluster-a, i-1).

MECHANICAL Schema-Write Conformance: no writeResource call site changed — cluster/instance rows are built exactly as before and their strict z.object schemas are untouched. The new scan_error twin file exists only to satisfy the aws_credentials twin's import; this model stores no scan_error spec and writes none.
cluster/instance keep lifetime 'infinite' and garbageCollection 10 — unchanged.
Read-only inventory model, not a CRUD lifecycle model. Unchanged by this slice.

No mutating methods and no SSO pre-flight in this package (single-region, ambient-only by design; typically gated by @jentz/aws-context-guard upstream in a workflow). This slice deliberately does not add one.
MECHANICAL: clusterKey/instanceKey unchanged ('cluster-<id>' vs 'instance-<cluster>--<id>', collision-free, pinned by tests). No new writes introduced.
writeResource-only producer; no readResource paths changed. The companion report's contract (cluster/instance rows) is byte-identical in shape.

model.version bumped 2026.06.26.0 -> 2026.07.03.0 with a matching no-op upgrade entry appended (identity upgradeAttributes; description names the retired app-level retry layer, no globalArguments schema changes). Chain tail equals model.version, verified by the colocated upgrade-chain test.
